title: Living in Boulder vlog 10 -  22 -  2018: History Books

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a late-night vlog update on his personal website project, which involves formatting and linking history books and classic texts (Plato, Aristotle, Josephus) for personal study. He discusses a detour into genealogy research tracing ancestors to Irish kings and Adam and Eve, shares his philosophy on memorizing books by breaking them into small chunks, and demonstrates how he uses the iOS text-to-speech feature to have books read aloud on his website.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Make yourself smarter than those in control and beat them with intellect
If people won't follow, give up on politics and become a philosopher — the truer pursuit
Break books into small chunks and assault them from all sides rather than reading cover to cover
Prefers to focus on intellectual work now while young; will exercise later when the website is done
Found errors in ancestral lines on Geni.com and chose to wait 10 years rather than fight the bad data
Wary of them after they corrupted chapter numbers on his website

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He hasn't done a video for two weeks.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The last time he did a video, he was working on the law code of America.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: His mom and fiancé reacted positively when he told them he was working on the law code of America.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He felt good about working on the law code, as if it was divinely inspired or internally rewarding.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He believes the best way to fix the world is to make yourself smarter than those in control and defeat them intellectually.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He plans to offer the U.S. law code as a downloadable file for people to modify themselves.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He suggests marking up the law code in red, running for office, and presenting the marked-up version as a demonstration of higher intellect.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: If people don't follow along with his approach, he believes it's their problem and one should then give up on politics and become a philosopher.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He abandoned the law code project because he realized he wasn't done with other tasks, like fixing books on his website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He discovered that many links on his website were broken and chapters were unnumbered due to a batch edit error.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He has been manually fixing the chapter numbers and links on his website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He uses a backup on his computer to restore missing dates or information.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Batch edits in Dreamweaver sometimes don't work as expected.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He traced his ancestry back to Adam and Eve using a website called Jenny.com (G-E-N-I.com).

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He found discrepancies in his ancestry records, such as incorrect timelines, and decided to give up for now.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He believes most people are directly related to famous kings and queens due to historical reproductive patterns.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: One of his ancestors lived from the 1500s and lived to be 111 years old.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He is currently working on attaching page numbers to 2,600 chapters in his book 'Plenty'.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He plans to finish the edits on 'Plenty' soon.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He encountered issues with batch editing timestamps for Josephus' 'Jewish Wars'.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He wants to study and memorize Aristotle's 'Politics' and Plato's 'Republic' to make a video about monarchy.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He finds it hard to focus on studying these books because he's high.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He plans to listen to these books repeatedly to understand them better.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He wants to title all the chapters in these books on his website, as they are currently only numbered.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He adds books to his website primarily for his own use, to have them formatted the way he prefers.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He believes memorizing books involves breaking them into smaller parts and associating them with visuals.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He criticized the idea of reading a book once and never revisiting it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He was called 'fat fuck' in a comment but doesn't prioritize exercise because he values intellectual work over physical fitness for now.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He believes in enjoying his youth by focusing on his website work, as physical health may decline later.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He wants to change the world through his work before focusing on personal enjoyment like exercise.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He estimates it will take him about three years to finish his current projects.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He wants to make a video about monarchy after studying Plato and Aristotle.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He demonstrates how to use his website to have books read aloud on an iPhone.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He finds it frustrating when links on his website don't work during demonstrations.
